_meta.description: Comprehensive education prestige ontology for resume feedback scoring. Scores are additive boosts (0.0–1.0 scale) applied on top of base candidate evaluation. Tier labels: ELITE (top ~20 global), PRESTIGE (top 100 global), STRONG (top 300 global), GOOD (top 500 / nationally respected), REGIONAL (nationally known, regionally respected), ACCREDITED (legitimate, lesser-known), UNRANKED (no verifiable ranking), NON_TRADITIONAL (bootcamps, MOOCs, self-study).
_meta.scoringNotes: baseBoost is added to a candidate's raw score before normalisation. degreeOverrides replace baseBoost when a specific degree at that institution is exceptionally strong or weak. All scores assume honest, verifiable credentials.

ELITE.label: Elite / World Top 20
ELITE.recruitingSignal: Immediate credibility signal to senior engineers and FAANG-tier recruiters. Filters rarely apply.

PRESTIGE.label: Prestigious / World Top 100
PRESTIGE.recruitingSignal: Strong positive signal. No filtering concerns at any company tier.

STRONG.label: Strong / World Top 300
STRONG.recruitingSignal: Well regarded. Degree quality and GPA become important differentiators.

GOOD.label: Good / World Top 500 or nationally respected
GOOD.recruitingSignal: Solid foundation. Projects, skills, and work experience carry more weight at this tier.

REGIONAL.label: Regional / nationally known
REGIONAL.recruitingSignal: Accredited, respectable. Degree level and field of study are key.

ACCREDITED.label: Accredited / lesser-known
ACCREDITED.recruitingSignal: Legitimate. Candidate must rely on skills and experience to differentiate.
examples: distance learning institutions with full accreditation

UNRANKED.label: Unranked / unverifiable
UNRANKED.recruitingSignal: Treat with caution. Flag for verification before advancing candidate.

NON_TRADITIONAL.label: Non-traditional / alternative pathway
NON_TRADITIONAL.recruitingSignal: Context-dependent. Portfolio and demonstrable skills are the primary signal.

feedback.note: World #1 in QS 2025. Exceptional signal for any technical role. No caveats.
Silicon Valley's home institution. Exceptional signal, particularly for AI/ML and startup roles.
#1 in THE Reputation Rankings 2025 for 14 consecutive years. Strong for business, law, sciences; CS programme also world-class.
#1 THE World Rankings 2025 for 9th consecutive year. Universally recognised. Strong for ML research (DeepMind ties).
Consistent top 5 globally. Computer Laboratory has produced foundational CS research. Strong for backend, compilers, systems.
QS World #2 in 2026. Strong STEM focus, excellent employer reputation especially in finance and deep tech.
Europe's top technical university. Excellent signal for robotics, systems, and ML research roles in Europe.
Top-tier CS-specific reputation, often ranked #1 US CS programme. School of Computer Science is a major signal for FAANG and ML roles.
Ivy League with top-10 CS programme. Strong signal for US market. Cornell Tech campus has growing NYC tech presence.
Ivy League. Strong NYC industry connections, especially for finance, media, and AI. Growing CS department reputation.
QS top 10. Home of DeepMind research links. Strong signal in European market for ML and AI roles.
World-leading informatics school. One of Europe's top AI research centres. Strong for NLP and ML roles.
Birthplace of deep learning (Hinton). Exceptional AI pedigree. Very strong signal for ML/AI roles globally.
Asia's top university by QS. Strong signal for APAC roles. Excellent for systems, networks, and AI.

Ireland's highest-ranked university (QS #75, 2026). Only Irish member of League of European Research Universities. Strong signal across EU and for multinational tech companies with Irish hubs (Google, Meta, Apple).
Ireland's largest university, QS #118 (up 8 places in 2026). Michael Smurfit Graduate Business School is world-renowned. Strong employer reputation in Ireland, UK, and EU.
QS #246, up 27 places in 2026. Top-50 European university by THE. Strong for pharma-tech, sustainability, and food science adjacent tech roles in Ireland.

QS #284. Member of Coimbra Group (with TCD). Named Sunday Times University of the Year 2022. Top 100 in Europe. Well-recognised by Irish/EU multinational tech employers. Strong AI and biomedical research programmes.

QS #401, up 20 places in 2026. Pioneered cooperative education (co-op) in Ireland. 15% higher than national average graduate employment rate. Strong industry links especially in mid-west tech cluster.
QS #410, up 11 places in 2026. Ireland's first university to integrate workplace internships into undergrad degrees. Strong industry-facing STEM focus.
QS 771-780, now in global top 800. Respected for arts, social sciences, and niche CS research. Smaller tech profile vs TCD/UCD but solid.
QS 781-790. Ireland's largest university by enrolment. Strong STEM and applied tech focus. Good for entry-level and mid-level tech roles in Ireland.
Formed from merger of several Irish IoTs in 2022. Respectable regional institutions with strong applied focus. Well regarded locally; less recognised internationally.
Formed from CIT and IT Tralee merger. Strong applied and industry-linked programmes in the Munster region.
Regional Irish TU covering the south-east. Solid applied computing programmes.
QS top 70. Russell Group. Strong UK employer recognition. Good for fintech and tech consulting.
QS #34, Russell Group. One of the UK's strongest CS departments. Joined THE top 50 reputation rankings 2025.
QS top 40. Russell Group with strong London employer network. Notable for AI ethics, security.
Russell Group, QS top 60. Strong robotics and computer vision research. Good UK employer signal.
Russell Group, QS top 80. Long CS history. Strong Scottish tech sector pipeline.
Premier German technical university in Europe's AI hub (Berlin). Strong for backend, distributed systems, AI.
QS top 80. Joined THE top 50 reputation 2025. One of Europe's strongest research universities. Strong AI and security labs.
Europe's #2 technical university after ETH Zurich. Excellent global research reputation. Strong AI, systems, and distributed systems.
QS top 30. Canada's most internationally recognised brand. Strong AI heritage (MILA adjacent).
QS top 40. Vancouver tech hub. Strong for software engineering and AI. Excellent employability in APAC and North American markets.
Joined THE top 50 reputation 2025. France's most recognised brand. Strong for theoretical CS and mathematics.
#8 in THE Reputation Rankings 2025. China's top technical university. Strong global signal especially for APAC-facing roles and AI research.
India's most globally recognised technical brand. BTech CS from any IIT is a very strong signal for US and EU technical roles.
Top-tier IIT. Strong employer recognition across US, UK, and Ireland tech multinationals.
Australia's highest QS-ranked university. THE top 50 reputation 2025. Strong for APAC and European roles.

match: Doctor of Philosophy in Computer Science

feedback.note: Exceptional signal for research roles, staff engineer, principal engineer, and FAANG L6+ tracks. May be neutral-to-negative for product-focused or startup roles if not paired with engineering experience.
feedback.caveats: Verify publication record and supervisor reputation for research roles. A PhD without strong practical experience is not a substitute for 5+ years of industry SWE.

feedback.note: Among the most valuable credentials in the current job market. Extremely strong signal for ML engineer, research scientist, and AI leadership roles.
feedback.caveats: Confirm publication quality (NeurIPS, ICML, ICLR, CVPR tier preferred).

match: Master of Science in Computer Science

feedback.note: Strong foundation signal. Combined with strong institution or internship experience, opens mid-level+ roles. Research-based MSc carries higher signal than coursework-only.
feedback.caveats: Distinguish between thesis-based (research signal) and coursework-only (skills signal).

match: Master of Science in Artificial Intelligence

feedback.note: High-demand credential. Strong signal for ML/AI roles. Verify curriculum depth: look for coverage of deep learning, probabilistic models, and research methods.
feedback.caveats: Market saturation increasing. Differentiate with projects, publications, or internships.

match: Master of Science in Data Science

feedback.note: Strong for data engineering, analytics engineering, and ML roles. Less so for pure SWE or backend roles. Portfolio work is critical.
feedback.caveats: Verify whether curriculum is statistics-heavy or engineering-heavy — affects role fit.

feedback.note: Solid practical credential. Strong for SWE, DevOps, and platform roles. Signal is strong at ELITE/PRESTIGE institutions (e.g. CMU MSE), moderate elsewhere.
Very strong in current market given talent shortage. Excellent for AppSec, cloud security, SOC, and GRC roles.
feedback.caveats: Certifications (CISSP, CEH, OSCP) alongside degree significantly boost signal.

match: Master of Science in Data Analytics

feedback.note: Good for analytics, BI, and junior data science. Less strong than MSc Data Science or MSc CS for pure engineering roles.
feedback.caveats: Portfolio of real data work is essential to convert this into interview invites.

feedback.note: Integrated master's common in UK. Strong signal, especially from Russell Group / top Irish universities. Often more rigorous than standalone MSc.

match: Bachelor of Science in Computer Science

feedback.note: Foundational credential for SWE roles. Combined with strong institution, internships, and projects, fully competitive for junior/mid-level roles at top companies.
feedback.caveats: At senior levels (5+ YOE), degree fades in importance relative to track record.

match: Bachelor of Science in Software Development

feedback.note: More applied than BSc CS. Good for SWE roles at product companies and SMEs. Strong portfolio work is important.
Solid for sysadmin, IT operations, and junior dev roles. Perceived as less rigorous than BSc CS by selective tech employers.
feedback.caveats: Supplement with technical certifications (AWS, GCP, CKAD) for cloud/infra roles.

feedback.note: Strong signal for quantitative roles: ML engineer, data scientist, quant, algorithmic trading. Highly regarded at top institutions for CS roles if paired with self-taught engineering.
feedback.caveats: Must pair with practical coding portfolio to be competitive for SWE roles.

feedback.note: Good for data science, analytics, and actuarial-adjacent tech roles. Strong at PRESTIGE institutions.
feedback.caveats: Programming portfolio required for ML/data engineering roles.

feedback.note: Strong for embedded systems, hardware, IoT, and robotics SWE roles. Less relevant for pure web dev or product SWE.

Strong signal for product management, engineering management, and technical sales. Neutral-to-negative for individual contributor engineering roles. ELITE MBA (Wharton, HBS, LBS, INSEAD, Smurfit) adds significant prestige.
feedback.caveats: Boost is highly institution-dependent. Smurfit (UCD) carries strong Irish/EU employer signal.

feedback.note: Strong for UX engineering, design systems, and product design roles. Carnegie Mellon HCII MSc is world-leading in this field.

match: Master of Science in Biomedical Engineering

feedback.note: Strong in Ireland's medical device sector (Galway is a world-leading med-device cluster). High relevance for ML in healthcare, medical imaging, and embedded health systems.

Common in Ireland as an accelerated conversion pathway (typically 1 year). Reputable Irish universities (TCD, UCD, UCC, UoG, DCU) deliver these well. Strong signal when backed by internship or project work.
feedback.caveats: Employers will probe whether it was a genuine conversion (sufficient CS depth) vs a credential. GitHub portfolio matters greatly here.

feedback.note: Common in US community college system. Valid entry point but most competitive roles expect a bachelor's. Flag for roles above junior level.
Strong cloud signal. Associate-level is a baseline; Professional-level adds meaningfully more.
High value for data engineering and MLOps roles.
Strong DevOps/platform signal. Highly sought after by cloud-native employers.
Strongest general InfoSec credential. Significant boost for security roles. Requires 5 YOE to maintain credibility.
Elite penetration testing credential. Very strong signal for offensive security, red team, and AppSec roles.
Modest signal. Less important than a strong ML portfolio or publication.
Valued for technical project/programme management roles. Negligible for IC SWE roles.

Named top-tier bootcamps with CIRR-verified or industry-recognised outcomes. Acceptable for junior roles at most tech employers. Employer acceptance has risen significantly: ~90% of employers report open to hiring bootcamp grads (2025). Flag for senior roles (L5+/Staff+) unless candidate has strong portfolio and YOE.
feedback.mitigation: Strong GitHub portfolio, open-source contributions, or a capstone project with measurable impact substantially offset bootcamp concerns.

feedback.note: Generic bootcamp without named institution. Cannot assess programme quality. Treat as unverified until name confirmed. For junior roles, focus evaluation on portfolio, GitHub activity, and interview performance. Not disqualifying — nearly 70% of employers still consider bootcamp grads qualified for tech roles.
feedback.mitigation: Verify programme name, length (prefer 12+ weeks full-time), and any CIRR or outcome data. Ask candidate for project demos.

feedback.note: MOOCs show initiative and continuous learning. Not equivalent to formal education but valued as supplementary. deeplearning.ai and fast.ai carry higher ML-specific signal. Best placed in a 'Continuous Learning' resume section rather than Education.
feedback.mitigation: Portfolio, GitHub, and applied projects are far stronger signals than MOOC completion alone.

feedback.note: Self-taught engineers can and do succeed at top companies. Signal strength is entirely dependent on portfolio quality, open-source contributions, and demonstrable past work.
feedback.mitigation: GitHub profile, open-source contributions, live production projects, or prior tech employment are essential to advance this candidate.

feedback.note: Increasingly respected pathway especially in UK and Ireland. Degree apprenticeships (combining employer-based training with university credit) are particularly strong. Strong signal for operational and junior engineering roles.

Significant open-source contributions (merged PRs in major projects, maintainer roles) are highly regarded by engineering hiring managers. This can compensate for weak formal education at many top-tier companies.

fieldRelevanceMultipliers.description: Multipliers applied to baseBoost based on how relevant the degree field is to the target role. These are multiplicative.

educationLevelWeights.description: Base weight applied before institution and degree-specific boosts. Reflects industry-standard expectations for experience-level transitions.

DOCTORAL.notes: Research signal. Exceptional for L6+ IC and research roles.

POSTGRADUATE.notes: Standard for senior IC, strong for all mid-level roles.

POSTGRADUATE_INTEGRATED.notes: MEng/integrated MSc typically viewed as stronger than coursework MSc.

CONVERSION.notes: HDip / PGDip conversions are valid but signal depth needs verification.

UNDERGRADUATE.notes: Foundational. Sufficient for most junior and many mid-level roles.

SUB_UNDERGRADUATE.notes: Associate / HNC-level. Flag for roles above junior.

NON_TRADITIONAL.notes: Portfolio-dependent. Interview performance and projects carry more weight.

redFlags.feedback: Institution not verifiable in any major accreditation database. Pause candidate and verify before proceeding.
Degree field has low relevance to applied role. Increase emphasis on practical skills evaluation and portfolio review.
Incomplete formal education. Evaluate based on years of industry experience and portfolio. Not disqualifying for experienced candidates.
Very short bootcamp duration (<8 weeks). Insufficient time to develop engineering fundamentals. Weight portfolio and live coding performance more heavily.

greenFlags.feedback: Peer-reviewed publication at a top venue is a significant positive signal, especially for ML/AI roles.
Research experience strengthens academic credentials and signals analytical depth.
High academic performance is a positive differentiator, especially at top institutions.
Competitive scholarship signals peer selection and is a green flag.
Top-tier tech internship is a strong signal that partially compensates for a weaker institution.

scoringFormula.description: Final education score computation. All components capped at 1.0.
scoringFormula.formula: educationScore = min(1.0, institutionBaseBoost * fieldRelevanceMultiplier + educationLevelWeight + greenFlagAdditives - redFlagPenalties)
scoringFormula.normalisationNote: educationScore feeds into compositeResumeScore alongside workExperienceScore, skillsScore, and projectsScore, typically weighted at 15-25% of total depending on seniority level.
